---
title: "Bounded pipeline fanout"
engine: pi-bio
format: gfm
---

`runPipeline` drains ordered tasks through a fixed number of worker lanes. This is the local topology underlying
bounded annotation batches; HTTP retry and response materialization remain the responsibility of the DuckNNG fanout
resolver.

```{ts .pi-bio}
import assert from "node:assert/strict";
import { runPipeline } from "../../dist/core/pipeline.js";

const concurrency = 3;
const chunks = Array.from({ length: 9 }, (_, index) => ({
  id: `chunk-${index}`,
  variants: [
    { af: index % 4 === 0 ? null : 0.0001 * (index + 1), highImpact: index % 2 === 0 },
    { af: 0.2, highImpact: true },
    { af: null, highImpact: false },
  ],
}));

let inFlight = 0;
let maxInFlight = 0;
const completionOrder = [];
const rows = await runPipeline(chunks, async (chunk, index) => {
  inFlight += 1;
  maxInFlight = Math.max(maxInFlight, inFlight);
  await new Promise((resolveDelay) => setTimeout(resolveDelay, Math.max(10, 70 - index * 8)));
  completionOrder.push(chunk.id);
  inFlight -= 1;
  return {
    chunk: chunk.id,
    included: chunk.variants.filter((variant) => variant.highImpact && variant.af !== null && variant.af < 0.01).length,
    abstained: chunk.variants.filter((variant) => variant.af === null).length,
  };
}, concurrency);

assert.ok(maxInFlight <= concurrency);
assert.deepEqual(rows.map((row) => row.chunk), chunks.map((chunk) => chunk.id));
assert.deepEqual([...completionOrder].sort(), chunks.map((chunk) => chunk.id).sort());
const completedOutOfOrder = completionOrder.some((id, index) => id !== rows[index].chunk);
assert.equal(completedOutOfOrder, true);

piBio.json({
  pattern: "bounded-pipeline",
  concurrency,
  maxInFlight,
  completedOutOfOrder,
  returnedOrder: rows.map((row) => row.chunk),
  totals: {
    included: rows.reduce((sum, row) => sum + row.included, 0),
    abstained: rows.reduce((sum, row) => sum + row.abstained, 0),
  },
});
```

The assertions establish bounded concurrency and stable task-order results despite out-of-order completion. They do
not establish endpoint rate-limit behavior; the DuckNNG fanout tests and VEP application cover that contract.
